name: @chbrain/khai-engine-hierarchy
description: khai engine content, the hierarchy domain: hierarchy as position, where a persona stands right now -- the rank the room reads on them in the field they are in.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Hierarchy as position: where a persona stands right now -- the rank the room reads on them when they enter the field they are in.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_hierarchy.md) declares standing as a rank the field assigns (not a trait the persona owns): a read produced by the persona's capital configuration and by the title, if any, the structure assigns. The law makes every persona carry a read.
card.issue: Nine expressions a persona may hold. Six capital positions read what the room makes of the persona's capital in this field -- dominant, established, ascending, declining, excluded, misplaced -- and three title positions name what a structure assigns -- superior, peer, subordinate. Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor; the rank is field-relative, so the same persona reads differently in different rooms.
card.require: Two altitudes must hold. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, so the rank read holds system-wide and no persona enters a field unranked. The link: each persona carries its read under Projection -- at least one capital position, plus a title position where the field assigns title.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and each persona links a real hierarchy position under Projection. Whether the read is true to the field and the scene is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give the persona its read under Projection: the capital position the current field reads on it, and the title position the structure assigns, each carrying the anchor upward. The field is named by the scene; read the rank in the first beat, hold it under every exchange.
